teachingHint: Your child is practising words starting with the consonant cluster /bl/. Two consonant sounds sit next to each other at the start: b then l. Help them blend through: b-l-a-ck → black. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: b-l-a-ck → Blend the opening cluster: bl-a-ck → black
b-l-o-b → Say each sound: /b/-/l/-/o/-/b/. Blend: blob

b-l-o-ck → Blend the opening cluster: bl-o-ck → block

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! The 'o' says 'u' and the 'e' is silent. We say 'cum'. Learn it by sight.

story: Come and see the black blob on the block! The blob did a little blink and went splat on the block.

teachingHint: Your child is practising words starting with the consonant cluster /bl/. Two consonant sounds sit next to each other at the start: b then l. Help them blend through: b-l-e-d → bled. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

b-l-a-g → Say each sound: /b/-/l/-/a/-/g/. Blend: blag

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! It looks like 'paid' but we say 'sed'. Learn it by sight.

story: She said there was a blot of ink that bled on the paper. It was bliss to blag a new pen!

story: Some of the blimp went with a blast! It made me blush to blend in with the blunt end of the crash.

teachingHint: Your child is practising words starting with the consonant cluster /cl/. Two consonant sounds sit next to each other at the start: c then l. Help them blend through: c-l-a-p → clap. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! The 'a' doesn't say its usual sound and the 'e' is silent. We say 'hav'. Learn it by sight.

story: Have a look at the clock! Clap for the clam with a clip on its shell. The clock went tick and the clam did a clap!

story: I like this club! A clod of mud fell on my clog and made a clot. The club had to get a mop for the clog.

story: I drank some milk and the cup was drunk dry! My plant began to droop so I gave it some too. Then I had a dream that I drank from a big jug!

story: We were looking in the trunk of a big tree. I trod on a branch and set a new trend! You can trust that the trunk was strong and I trod with care.
